scope_comment: App-level mutation testing for the design integration's new pure logic
(DESIGN-INTEGRATION-DECISIONS item 6, >= 80): theme resolution
(src/ui/themeResolve.ts), color mixing (src/ui/mixColor.ts), pure shell
logic (src/ui/shell/navActive.ts, profileClaims.ts), the new pure
feature formatting/grouping libs (transactions sections/display, goals
totals, budget amounts incl. the spec-named preset computation,
dashboard heroMath/labels, reports labels), and the Phase 9 motion math
(src/ui/motion/motionMath.ts kill-switch/duration rules + countUpMath.ts
digit-roll slicing; motion/tokens.ts is pure data, covered by typecheck).
NOT mutated here, with reasons:
- src/ui/charts/* and src/i18n/*: covered by their own stryker configs
(run by the same `npm run test:mutation`).
- React/RN bindings (*.tsx, hooks like useDueLabel/useChartEntrance,
useT, systemLocale): render/IO code, covered by typecheck plus the
integration/e2e layers of the test plan.
- features/budget/lib/colors.ts: 12-line delegation to the chart kit's
categoryColor (mutation-tested in the charts config); importing the
charts index would drag RN components into the node harness.
- pre-redesign pure modules unchanged by the integration (src/lib/dates,
transactions dateRanges, goals inputs/projection, budget grouping,
recurring upcoming, reports series): outside the decision-6 'new pure
logic' scope; goals inputs/projection still compile into the sandbox
because the goals harness emits them alongside totals.
runner_comment: Same harness pattern as packages/shared and the charts/i18n configs:
tests are node:test files, so the tap runner executes each compiled test
file with `node` and parses the TAP stream. buildCommand compiles every
per-package instrumented harness once in the sandbox (mutant switching
does the rest); each tsconfig.stryker.json is self-contained because the
sandbox copy of app/ breaks the ../tsconfig.base.json extends chain.
thresholds_comment: break 75 / target >= 80 per the integration quality bar; the score is
driven to >= 80 by strengthening tests, never by lowering thresholds.
sandbox_comment: Every tsconfig except the self-contained *.stryker.json builds is
excluded from the sandbox: they extend ../tsconfig.base.json (outside
the sandbox), and their presence triggers Stryker's tsconfig
preprocessor, which dynamically imports 'typescript' from the npx
install tree where it is not present. dist/ (expo export), dist-test/
(stale local builds), reports and assets are dead weight per sandbox.
